content:
my mother grew tired of me by the holidays, just 4 months later, and by May, I had to sneak out and drive back to Florida from NYC, to live with my father again, and quickly, I was turned into Cinderella; instead of help, I was burdened with the chores and tasks of a home containing 6 other people, when the only other chore holder was my stepmom; me being the only person in my family to hold a degree (BA & MFA), the only person with a real and purpose-driven, albeit community-benefiting dream, struggling with my weight my whole life, addiction, my sexuality (I was outed when I fell off the balcony by my roommate and at times boyfriend);

I was submissive to my father and the house and ate shit, and shoveled shit, and let everyone think I was a fan of their bigotry and condescension because, as always, I've been in pure survival mode; by November, I was run ragged, I exploded and told my father he was a criminal, and a terrible person, and that's how the cards collapsed; I didn't mean to do it like that, I didn't mean to blow up my life.

But now I'm back at my mother's, and she is an awful person as well, feeling terrible that I tried to kill myself and worried about me (ironic quotes abound), but demands I leave and render myself homeless because I don't roll over and let her shit on me every day without speaking up for myself, trying to preserve my energy and my current positive, sobering, emotional, and mental superpowers as I move closer and closer to my goals with an even further refined and distilled purpose and unity;
context: From document: Title: Surviving Collapse — chunk 2 (chunk 2/4)
